Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical findings for underlying metabolic or renal pathology.
